phenomenon: Sand-to-stars anthology: scale ladder from silanol bond to observable universe
key_measurement: Scale span: Si–O bond length 1.62 Å (1.62×10⁻¹⁰ m) to observable universe radius ~4.4×10²⁶ m — 36 orders of magnitude; each decade of scale corresponds to one rung on the Powers of Ten ladder
institutions: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) — CODATA physical constants
NASA Jet Propulsion Laboratory (Solar System scale reference)
ESA Planck Science Team (cosmic distance scale)
Eames Office / IBM (Powers of Ten film, 1977 — foundational scale-ladder reference)

sources.citation: NIST CODATA 2018 — Fundamental Physical Constants, National Institute of Standards and Technology
Morrison, P. & Morrison, P. (1982) 'Powers of Ten: A Book About the Relative Size of Things in the Universe and the Effect of Adding Another Zero', Scientific American Books / W.H. Freeman, New York
Planck Collaboration: Aghanim, N. et al. (2020) 'Planck 2018 results. VI. Cosmological parameters', Astronomy & Astrophysics 641:A6

visualization_compliance: Every scale transition frame must display an explicit log₁₀ label (e.g., '10⁻⁹ m — nanometer scale') — no unlabeled scale jumps
Scale compressions spanning more than 3 orders of magnitude in a single cut must be annotated with a 'SCALE JUMP ×N' overlay
All phenomena depicted must cross-reference their source subject file (S001–S009) by subject_id in the metadata layer
Do not merge phenomena from different scale rungs into a single composite frame without explicit scale-discontinuity labeling
Studio handoff pack must include: subject_id cross-reference table, scale bar master template, and principle_set flag for each rung
Observable universe boundary must be labeled 'COMOVING RADIUS ~46 Gly — not a physical edge' to avoid implying a hard boundary
Each inter-episode transition must carry a STUDIO HANDOFF label with the receiving subject_id and scale context
